category_title: Adolescent readers who internalize institutional 'struggling' labels despite high engagement in self-selected reading
student_description: This student actively resists traditional, required reading tasks and internalizes negative institutional labels like 'slow reader' or 'ADHD' when faced with time constraints, uninteresting texts, or group work. They uniquely require complete autonomy in text selection, self-paced reading without time pressure, and authentic one-on-one conversations to build engagement and reading efficiency.

criteria.rationale: Allowing the student to select their own reading material prevents the immediate shutdown and skimming behaviors triggered by assigned texts.
criteria.check: The tutor offers the student alternative book options or the freedom to choose what they read, rather than forcing compliance with a specific assigned text.

criteria.rationale: Removing time limits reduces the pressure that causes this student to panic and identify as a 'slow reader'.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly removes time limits or speed expectations, allowing the student to read entirely at their own pace.

criteria.rationale: Scripted decoding and fluency drills reinforce the student's negative self-image and must be avoided.
criteria.check: The tutor does not introduce scripted decoding exercises, fluency drills, or speed-reading tasks in their response.
